policy.summary: Boundary-crossing features must have e2e or integration tests for the actual API, CLI, service, adapter, file format, generated artifact, workflow, storage, or user-facing boundary.

boundaries.required_behaviour: Command review CLI parses real options, writes redacted audit records, opens approval tickets, respects dry-run/execute modes, and preserves single-use approval semantics.

evidence_tests.covers: run_guard/main paths, audit JSONL persistence, approval-store persistence, execution gating, console-script declaration

boundaries.required_behaviour: Operator approval CLI lists, shows, approves, denies, rejects invalid ticket state, and preserves an existing queue when a durable replacement fails.

evidence_tests.covers: CLI main paths over ApprovalQueue JSON store including unknown/already-decided digests
ApprovalQueue JSON store persistence, single-use approval semantics, and atomic-replacement failure preservation

boundaries.required_behaviour: Policy CLI reads profile TOML and case JSON, persists governance JSON, exposes candidate threshold and capability/origin changes, detects drift, approves, denies, and opens rollback proposals that require second-reviewer approval before the head changes.

evidence_tests.covers: status/propose/approve/deny/expose/drift, capability-aware exposure, and approval-gated rollback CLI flows with real temp files

boundaries.required_behaviour: Audit decisions append to hash-chained JSONL, verify across restart/tamper cases, and export redacted SIEM JSONL through API and CLI entry points.

evidence_tests.covers: append, verify, mutation/reorder/deletion/truncation/corrupt-line detection, sidecar handling
verified chain to SIEM JSONL export and malformed detector-list handling
CLI stdout/file export and fail-closed invalid-chain handling

boundaries.required_behaviour: Approval HTTP service protects endpoints, handles pending/approve/deny requests over real loopback HTTP, emits webhook events, and rejects malformed bodies or invalid operator keys.

evidence_tests.covers: ThreadingHTTPServer loopback requests, ApprovalQueue persistence, service errors, webhook sink validation

boundaries.required_behaviour: In-process MCP gateway reviews discovery, signed registrations, populated registration manifests, tool calls, remote auth, capability policy, approved policy-store heads, runtime argument-schema conformance, cross-server lookalike tools, and tool responses without executing tools or leaking raw argument values.

evidence_tests.covers: discovery, signed call review, under-populated registration hard blocks, cross-server lookalike hard blocks, argument-schema violation hard blocks, remote auth fail-closed, capability policy, response review, redacted audit events
approved Guardrail-as-Code head compiled into MCP gateway fusion and capability policy
structured MCP call inspection, registry checks, argument taint, traversal/exfiltration, optional Rust parity fallback
registration signatures, under-populated manifest rejection, schema drift, cross-server NFKC/confusable/edit-distance lookalike names, runtime argument-schema conformance, dynamic discovery policy

boundaries.required_behaviour: Loopback MCP service exposes health/discovery/review/response endpoints, binds reviews to an approved policy store, authenticates protected routes, preserves redaction, and rejects malformed or oversized requests.

evidence_tests.covers: real loopback HTTP server discovery/review/health/error routes, policy-store binding, and operator-key handling

boundaries.required_behaviour: MCP gateway CLI parses runtime flags including policy-store binding, builds loopback/non-loopback servers safely, exposes health, and closes servers on shutdown paths.

evidence_tests.covers: CLI option parsing, policy-store option, loopback binding, operator-key requirement, server lifecycle, console-script declaration

boundaries.required_behaviour: SDK middleware renders tool actions deterministically, reviews without default execution, executes permitted calls exactly once, blocks untrusted mutations, routes approval through a durable digest-scoped queue when configured, and emits digest-only audit events to the hash-chained audit sink when configured.

evidence_tests.covers: review/run paths, dry-run behaviour, executor override, durable approval queue route, hash-chain audit sink, redacted audit event

boundaries.required_behaviour: Authorised destructive shell workflow composes queue, Governor, and shell effector so first review opens a ticket, explicit approval permits one execution, and replay is blocked.

evidence_tests.covers: full approval-to-effector workflow with real queue path and injected executor
shell/MCP effector dry-run, block, escalation, approval, subprocess guard semantics

boundaries.required_behaviour: Policy governance state serialises/deserialises profiles, proposals, revisions, append-only lineage, approval-gated rollback proposals, threshold and capability-aware exposure, and drift detection without accepting tampered lineage.

evidence_tests.covers: governance JSON save/load, unsupported version, tampered lineage
append-only revision lineage and rollback semantics
propose/approve/deny/approval-gated rollback/expose/drift workflow and persisted reload
approved baseline comparison and emitted drift events

boundaries.required_behaviour: Evidence packages bind to audit-chain proofs, redact payloads, preserve capability/control mappings, and replay sanitized incident fixtures across allow/block routes.

evidence_tests.covers: package JSON, hash-chain proof, controls, capability projection, sanitized incident replay

boundaries.required_behaviour: External action-surface import validates manifest/source ledger/licence decisions, copies only reviewed JSONL, writes receipts, rejects replacement without flag, and rejects malformed rows.

evidence_tests.covers: CLI import path over manifest, source ledger, JSONL input, target JSONL, and receipt files
external-source manifest and ledger load/skip/reject behaviour
authored/external/private partition validation and claim-boundary metadata

boundaries.required_behaviour: Action benchmark assembles deterministic authored corpus, validates partitions, records run/evidence metadata, and keeps claim boundaries separate from local regression evidence.

evidence_tests.covers: benchmark result schema, route metrics, detector contract, corpus contract
seed plus catalogue assembly, schema validation, uniqueness, deterministic categories
evidence classification, run context JSON, Markdown claim boundary

boundaries.required_behaviour: Adaptive red-team replay accepts only curated cases, validates schema/enums, generates mutation families, and feeds replay-ready rows to the benchmark path.

evidence_tests.covers: adversarial benchmark cases and route outcomes through production benchmark helpers
schema rejection, mutation families, curator acceptance, replay readiness

boundaries.required_behaviour: Documentation surface checker validates docs/notebook/demo entry points, and the action checkpoint demo runs as a subprocess while keeping command text redacted.

evidence_tests.covers: copied docs surface validation plus subprocess execution of demos/action_checkpoint.py

boundaries.required_behaviour: Studio federation manifest builds deterministic schema-A JSON, preserves honest side-effect tiers, computes stable digests, and fails drift checks for missing or stale artifacts.

evidence_tests.covers: manifest schema, digest stability, side-effect tiers, public reexports
real tool file loaded from repository path, emit path, committed-artifact check, missing/stale drift failures

boundaries.required_behaviour: Clean-checkout local gates validate tracked repository readiness and Phase 4 intake JSON inputs, fail closed on stale CI/local-gate metadata, and enforce the documented 100% coverage gate without relying on ignored internal files.

evidence_tests.covers: repository-readiness CLI default, tracked spec path, BUSL/private package metadata, required remote blockers, CI job names, Makefile local gates, and 100% coverage-gate enforcement
Phase 4 intake CLI default, tracked ledger path, required task metadata, blocked-task disclosure, external-claim boundary, and duplicate-id rejection

boundaries.required_behaviour: Package metadata matches runtime version, declares py.typed and console scripts, and named CLI tests confirm entry-point declarations for operational commands.

evidence_tests.covers: pyproject metadata, package version, py.typed inclusion
director-class-guard console-script declaration
director-class-approve console-script declaration
director-class-mcp-gateway console-script declaration
director-class-siem-export console-script declaration

boundaries.required_behaviour: Browser/computer-use and long-horizon memory action signals preserve metadata, detect unsafe user-facing mutations, and keep malicious context from downgrading action-plane blocks.

evidence_tests.covers: browser action metadata, unsafe browser/computer-use actions, safe controls
memory context serialization, long-horizon malicious instruction signals, safe controls
causal timeline metadata and retrieved/tool-output takeover detection

boundaries.required_behaviour: Content/model-backed adapters run through injected production adapter contracts in tests, keep optional external model loading behind factories, emit real DetectorSignal objects, and redact sensitive values.

evidence_tests.covers: citation tracer adapter contract, uncited-claim signals, from_pretrained bridge boundary
LayeredPromptGuard backend contract, query/context scanning, redacted rationale
PII backend contract, category/span output, no raw sensitive values
contradiction, response NLI, and token-span adapter behaviour through EvaluationRequest
support-scorer contract and action/task divergence signals
judge spec, panel construction, optional model-backed detector boundary

boundaries.required_behaviour: Policy profiles and capability grants validate config, compile origin/action envelopes, expose candidate deltas, and keep review/revision workflows digest-bound.

evidence_tests.covers: built-in profile compilation and origin/capability envelope behaviour
grant matching, origin denial, approval-required routing, audit projection
candidate policy exposure case evaluation and route transition reporting
policy proposal approval/denial metadata and reviewer rules
revision digest, lineage, metadata, rollback provenance
policy exposure corpus row schema, detector-signal conversion, capability context, and capability grants

boundaries.required_behaviour: Runtime guard and SDK review use approved policy posture, reject missing or drifted runtime state, and preserve capability/origin rules across CLI and SDK boundaries.

evidence_tests.covers: guard policy resolution and SDK runtime posture binding through real governance store
strict guard rejection of missing approved heads and drifted live runtime profiles through real audit paths

boundaries.required_behaviour: Signal fusion and optional meta-classifier keep default fail-closed policy, serialise deterministic models, handle Rust fallback safely, and route action/content/integrity signals consistently.

evidence_tests.covers: feature extraction, fit, serialization, fusion adapter, optional Rust fallback
fail-closed fusion routes for content/integrity/action signals
tiered ensemble execution and short-circuit semantics
calibration policy applied to detector signals
